type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py
spec: Please add tests for my error_threshold feature to Tests/test_image_quantize.py. Spec: Image.quantize(colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None) gets a new keyword-only-by-default arg error_threshold (float, default None meaning disabled/current behavior unchanged). When error_threshold is set, quantize should attempt to use as few colors as possible (up to the 'colors' limit) such that the mean squared error (MSE) between the original image (converted to RGB for comparison) and the quantized+converted-back-to-RGB image is <= error_threshold. Implementation approach: try increasing color counts (e.g. via a doubling/binary search or incremental loop from a small number up to 'colors'), computing MSE for each candidate quantized image using something like: convert original to RGB, convert quantized result to RGB, compute mean of squared per-pixel per-channel differences (can use ImageChops.difference and mean of squared array, or simple pure python loop over sampled pixels for reasonable performance). Stop as soon as MSE <= error_threshold, and return that quantized image. If not achievable within 'colors' colors, just return the result quantized with the full 'colors' count (already computed on the last iteration). If error_threshold is None, behavior is exactly as before (no MSE computation, no extra overhead). Suggested tests to add: (1) test_error_threshold_basic - quantize an image with error_threshold=1000 and assert result.mode == 'P' and len(result.getcolors()) <= colors requested; (2) test_error_threshold_none - default behavior unchanged when error_threshold not passed; (3) test_error_threshold_uses_fewer_colors - on a simple/flat image (e.g. a solid color square or 2-color image), assert that quantizing with a generous error_threshold uses fewer colors than the requested 'colors' limit, e.g. colors used <= 4 when colors=256; (4) test_error_threshold_cannot_be_met - use an impossible-to-meet threshold like error_threshold=0.0001 on a complex/noisy image with a low colors limit (e.g. colors=4), and assert the result still returns a valid 'P' mode image using the max colors (4) without raising an error. Please name the parameter exactly 'error_threshold' and place it after 'dither' in the signature to match my Image.py changes. Let me know if you have questions before implementing.

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: I will own src/PIL/Image.py and implement both sort_palette (mine) and error_threshold (yours) in quantize(). Please own Tests/test_image_quantize.py.

type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py
spec: For my sort_palette feature: quantize() gains a new kwarg sort_palette=None (placed after dither param, default None = no change in behavior/ordering). Accepted values: None (no sorting, current behavior), 'luminosity' (sort palette entries by perceptual brightness, ascending: 0.299*R + 0.587*G + 0.114*B), 'rgb' (sort palette entries by (R,G,B) tuple ascending). When sort_palette is set, after quantization the returned image's palette colors are reordered accordingly and each pixel's index in the returned image is remapped to match the new order (image visually unchanged, only index/palette order changes). Please add tests in Tests/test_image_quantize.py, e.g. test_sort_palette_none (default behavior unchanged), test_sort_palette_luminosity (palette sorted ascending by luminosity after calling im.quantize(sort_palette='luminosity')), test_sort_palette_rgb (palette sorted ascending by RGB tuple), and test_sort_palette_invalid (invalid value raises ValueError). Use a sample RGB image such as hopper() from Tests/helper.py, quantize with colors=N, and assert palette entries are non-decreasing per the chosen key by reading im.getpalette() in chunks of 3, and assert visual equivalence via im.convert('RGB') pixel comparison against unsorted quantize (or comparing pixel color at same coordinates).
note: sort_palette tests

type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/Image.py
spec: Acknowledged - you own src/PIL/Image.py. Please implement error_threshold per the spec I sent: add error_threshold=None parameter to quantize() after dither. When set (float), incrementally/iteratively quantize with increasing color counts up to 'colors', computing MSE between original (as RGB) and quantized-then-converted-back-to-RGB result, stopping as soon as MSE <= error_threshold; if unmet even at 'colors' colors, just return the result quantized at 'colors'. When error_threshold is None, behavior must be identical to current code (no extra computation). Docstring: add ':param error_threshold: Maximum mean squared error (MSE) allowed between the original and quantized image. If set, quantize() will use the fewest colors (up to 'colors') needed to stay within this error, falling back to 'colors' colors if the threshold cannot be met.'
